trial_details.trajectory:
<result>
FIX COMPLETE

Diagnosis:
- The pod was stuck in the "ErrImageNeverPull" state, indicating that the container image specified in the pod definition was not available.
- The pod events showed that the image "misspeling-app:latset" could not be pulled.

Fix:
1. Patched the deployment to use the correct image name "myapp:latest".
2. Deleted the problematic pod to force a recreation with the corrected image.

The pod should now start successfully with the updated image.
</result>

## COMMANDS EXECUTED:

   Output: NAME                                      READY   STATUS              RESTARTS   AGE

Namespace:        case6-test
Priority:         0
Service Account:  default
Node:             minikube/192.168.49.2
Start Time:       Mon, 15 D...

Error from server (BadRequest): container "myapp" in pod "misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-4vms8" is waiting to start: ErrImageNeverPull...

   Output: LAST SEEN   TYPE      REASON              OBJECT                                         MESSAGE
31s         Normal    Scheduled           pod/misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-4vms8    Successfully a...

   Output: deployment.apps/misspelling-deployment patched...
6. $ kubectl delete pod misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-4vms8 -n case6-test
   Output: pod "misspelling-deployment-6579ff5454-4vms8" deleted...
